title: gen_id uses Relaxed atomic: IDs not monotonic under concurrent posts
description:
LOCATION: src/id.rs:16

FINDING: gen_id() uses AtomicU64::fetch_add with Ordering::Relaxed. The comment acknowledges "cross-thread IDs within the same millisecond may not be strictly monotonic." In practice, on x86 Relaxed is as strong as SeqCst for fetch_add, so monotonicity is incidental. However, the real issue is that the ID format is "{millis}-{seq:06}" where seq is global across all posts for all personas. On a busy server posting to multiple personas simultaneously from separate tokio tasks, two posts created in the same millisecond can get the same seq value if the Relaxed fetch_add is reordered by the compiler or CPU. More concretely: SQLite primary key is TEXT and uses INSERT, so duplicates would cause a UNIQUE constraint failure that propagates as an error to the caller. The fan_out loop in delivery.rs also calls gen_id() in a tight loop for each delivery row, with no gap between calls. If two tokio tasks call gen_id() concurrently at the same millisecond boundary with Relaxed ordering, they could get the same seq. On weakly-ordered architectures (ARM), this is not theoretical.

IMPACT: Duplicate primary key on a concurrent post burst causes post creation to fail with a DB error. The user sees a 500 from the webhook endpoint or a CLI error.
